channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Western Cape still counting the cost of last month's floods
item.description: In tonight's programme: South Africa's Western Cape is still counting the cost of last month's devastating floods. Also, in DR Congo, the government has reported a substantial daily jump in Ebola cases. Plus Ivorian fans are living large in the wake of their team's victorious first World Cup appearance in 12 years on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Georja CALVIN-SMITH

item.title: Mass rollout planned across Southern Africa for new HIV drug
item.description: Administered just twice a year, the drug Lenacapavir offers near-complete protection — more than 99% — against HIV. After years of research and clinical trials, the preventive treatment has been approved and is now set to be rolled out on a large scale. Mass rollout campaigns are planned across Southern Africa — South Africa, Zambia, Eswatini, Zimbabwe, Lesotho and Mozambique — the region hardest hit by HIV/AIDS. Our team on the ground reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hundreds still homeless one month on from South Africa's Western Cape floods
item.description: South Africa’s Western Cape is still taking stock after last month’s severe and fatal storms. 11 people were killed and tens of thousands of homes were damaged. The area is known for its wine cultivation and many of those displaced are farm workers whose accommodation on their employers’ land was completely wiped out. Mercia Andrews works with an outreach organisation that has been trying to support some of those displaced by the floods..
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France braces for another heatwave as temperatures soar
item.description: France is bracing for another heatwave with temperatures set to soar to 40 degrees celsius in parts of the country in the coming days. This heatwave is promising to be even more challenging than the last one in May.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump says Russia 'should make a deal' after G7 meeting with Zelensky
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday that "Russia should make a deal" to end its war against Ukraine, after meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the G7 summit in France. France 24's Philip Turle has the latest from the meeting in Évian-les-Bains.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Cape Verde cause upset, France to face Senegal
item.description: Spain were held to an unexpected draw as Cape Verde held on to secure a point in their first-ever World Cup game. The goalless result means the newcomers have a chance to reach the first knockout round while Spain will seek to bounce back from the shock in their next game against Saudi Arabia. Elsewhere, France prepare to face Senegal, but will coach Didier Deschamps unlease his attacking power?
item.dc:creator: James VASINA

item.title: G7 allies scramble to put Ukraine back atop Trump's agenda
item.description: US allies at the Group of Seven summit worked Tuesday to push the war in Ukraine back up the agenda of President Donald Trump after more than four years of fighting sparked by Russia’s full-scale invasion. The Iran conflict has in recent weeks overshadowed the war in Ukraine that Russian President Vladimir Putin launched. Host President Emmanuel Macron of France said he’ll seek to persuade Trump to continue supporting Ukraine and increase pressure on Russia to help reach a peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'A good feeling': How fans in Iran experienced World Cup opener
item.description: Iran twice came from behind to salvage a 2–2 draw against New Zealand in a World Cup opener overshadowed by geopolitical tensions, protests and months of conflict with the United States. In Tehran, the match was an opportunity for many to forget for a moment the turmoil in the country as they came together at watch parties to cheer on their team.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: South Africa marks 50 years since Soweto uprising
item.description: South Africa on Tuesday marks the 50th anniversary of the Soweto uprising, when over 200 young people protesting against the apartheid education system were shot and killed by the police.,
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup: France face Senegal looking to avoid repeat of 2002 shock
item.description: France get their 2026 World Cup campaign underway Tuesday when they take on Senegal in New York. Les Bleus will be looking to avoid a repeat of their 2002 World Cup match against her same opponents which saw them suffer a 1-0 shock defeat.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Flag debate divides diaspora as Iran begin World Cup with New Zealand draw
item.description: Iran kicked off their World Cup with a tense 2-2 draw against New Zealand in Los Angeles. Overcoming several hurdles sparked by the US-Iran war, the team's participation and representation in the Group G matches has been at the heart of multiple controversies, among them is FIFA's ban on the use of Iran's pre-revolutionary flag, which has left the Iranian community in Los Angeles divided.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: No toll but ‘maritime service fees’ for Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: Shipping remains virtually at a standstill through the Strait of Hormuz, according to tracking platforms. That's despite US President Donald Trump's claim that crossings were resuming under his deal to end the war with Iran. Meanwhile a row is emerging over Iran's plan to charge levies on shipping.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump threatens 100% tariff on French wines unless digital tax dropped
item.description: US President Donald Trump has threatened to set a 100 percent tariff on French wine and champagne unless Paris removes a digital services tax on technology firms. France imposed in 2019 a three percent levy on the revenues earned by technology firms - including US giants like Facebook, Amazon, Apple and Google's parent Alphabet - within the country's borders.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: North Korean hackers steal Balkan identities to freelance in Europe and US
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, June 16: Papers focus on the historic Pechersk Lavra monastery in Kyiv that was set on fire by a missile barrage blamed on Russia. Next: a French-Vietnamese woman is appealing against the companies that supplied the deadly Agent Orange chemical during the Vietnam War. Also: an investigation shows that North Korean hackers are stealing Balkan identities. Finally, a cat interrupts the final scene of "Romeo and Juliet" in a theatre in Turkey.
item.dc:creator: Elitsa GADEVA

item.title: Eldest son of Norway’s crown princess sentenced to 4 years in prison for rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway’s Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was sentenced to four years in prison Monday after being convicted of rape. He was found guilty of two of the four rape charges he was facing and acquitted of the other two.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: ‘We won’t leave children in the cold’: Lebanese monastery opens doors to displaced
item.description: More than a million people have been displaced by Israeli airstrikes and evacuation orders across Lebanon, with many of them left living in tents or on roadsides. But amid the horrors of war there are also touching acts of solidarity. Our reporters Elena Volochine and Antonia Kerrigan spent a day in a monastery which has opened its doors to the displaced from across the south of the country.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump says Iran deal signed but details remain unclear
item.description: US President Donald Trump said on Monday a preliminary agreement to end the conflict had already been signed by the US and Iran digitally, ahead of a formal signing ceremony in Switzerland on Friday. However, details of the deal remain unclear, with Trump taking to social media to deny some elements of the agreement that have already been reported in the media, including the existence of a reconstruction fund.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: G7 summit: A case of Zelenksy and Europe 'versus Trump'?
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday joined a summit of the G7 group of leading powers seeking to up pressure on Russia to end the war against his country. The summit will likely see Zelensky and European leaders join forces in attempt to persuade US President Donald Trump to increase support for Ukraine and up sanctions on Moscow, says France 24’s international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: France kick off campaign against Senegal
item.description: The French team kick off their World Cup campaign against Senegal this Tuesday. Les Bleus are keen to avoid a repeat of their opening match at the 2002 World Cup, when they succombed to a 1-0 defeat.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: World Cup 2026: Tunisia sacks coach Lamouchi, appoints Renard after thrashing by Sweden
item.description: Tunisia responded to their 5-1 World Cup thrashing against Sweden by sacking coach Sabri Lamouchi and replacing him with Frenchman Hervé Renard, the federation announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: India blocks Telegram over 'medical entrance exam fraud' concerns
item.description: India's government has blocked messaging app Telegram until ​June 22, saying the platform was used to "defraud candidates" taking the medical entrance examination. The restriction was issued under a stringent provision ​of the IT law, which empowers ‌the government to block access to online sites in the "interest of sovereignty and integrity of ‌India".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trial begins for former Central African Republic leader Bozizé over war crimes
item.description: The UN-backed Special Criminal Court in the Central African Republic will on Tuesday, with foreign judges, begin the trial in absentia of former president François Bozizé over crimes against humanity. Bozizé, 79, who seized power in a 2003 coup before being overthrown 10 years later by rebels, has been living in exile in Guinea-Bissau since March 2023.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France kick off World Cup campaign, with Senegal looking to repeat 2002 upset
item.description: Les Bleus will be wary of carrying the favourites tag when they begin their quest for a third World Cup title against Senegal on Tuesday, 24 years after their shock defeat against the Lions of Teranga set off dancing in the streets of Dakar – and precipitated France's calamitous exit in the group stage.
item.source: © Vincent Carchietta, IMAGN IMAGES via Reuters
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Iran held by New Zealand in World Cup opener overshadowed by politics
item.description: Iran twice came from behind to salvage a 2–2 draw against New Zealand in a World Cup opener overshadowed by geopolitical tensions, protests and months of conflict with the United States. The result leaves Team Melli still searching for a breakthrough on football's biggest stage.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
